purpose: Defines the !prompt_audit slash command — POST-MORTEM tool analyzing the last completed swarm's worker performance and prompt quality. Resolves dashboard (argument or assigned), reads initialization.json + all progress/ files + logs.json, then computes per-task metrics (template_version, duration, stage progression count, deviation count+severity, log density, status, milestone count, prompt_size), analyzes upstream-result completeness (GOOD if logs reference upstream task IDs, GAP if has deps but no evidence of reading upstream progress files, N/A if no deps), checks for convention filtering evidence in worker prompts, then assigns letter grades A-F per task based on completion+deviations+stage count+log density+upstream score. Outputs scorecard table + summary statistics + 2-5 recommendations triggered by thresholds (failure>20%, upstream gaps, log density<1/min, deviations>30%, no template_version coverage, CRITICAL deviations, prompt size >10K or <1K tokens). Read-only.
gotchas: POST-MORTEM only — runs after swarm completes; reports 'No swarm data to audit' if initialization.json.task is null.
Read all progress files in PARALLEL — they have no dependencies on each other.
Handle MISSING data gracefully — not all fields may be present in every progress file. Use null/N/A rather than failing.
Be CONSTRUCTIVE not punitive — frame recommendations as actionable improvements, not blame for past mistakes.
Convention filtering presence is INFERRED from worker prompt quality (it's an in-memory structure during planning, not a persisted file).
Letter grades are deterministic from collected metrics — A=clean, B=minor issues, C=2+ deviations or sparse logging, D=CRITICAL deviation or upstream GAP, F=failed.
Upstream score 'GAP' fires when task has dependencies but logs show no evidence of reading upstream progress files — surfaces a specific dispatch-prompt failure mode.
Generate ONLY recommendations that apply (min 2 max 5), prioritized by impact.
Log density formula: total log entries / duration in minutes. <1/min = low; <0.5/min = sparse.
patterns: Post-mortem dashboard analysis: read metadata → compute metrics → grade → recommend.
Cross-reference: progress files ↔ initialization.json by task_id.
